summary: Frontend smoke test of iteration_2 features. 14/15 checks passed. Roster folder nesting (create/rename/open/breadcrumb), ItemDialog active fields / available-fields-list / add+remove field / new-global-field creation, tier-filter-chips, paste-bar-link-btn, search/sort, and localStorage persistence after reload all work. Single failure is drag-character-into-folder via synthetic HTML5 DragEvent (Corvo remained at root after dispatched dragstart/drop) — this is likely a test-environment limitation (DataTransfer.setData payload does not persist across dispatched DragEvents in Chromium headless), not a confirmed product bug. Code review of CharacterSelect.jsx shows drop handlers read 'application/x-roster' payload correctly, so manual drag should work.
passed: App loads at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with character-select view
open-folder-<name> and breadcrumb-up navigate in/out of folders
Inside folder shows characters scoped to that folder
add-field-to-item-Value moves field to active (item-dialog-field-Value visible)
item-dialog-remove-field-Value returns field to available list
new-global-field-input + new-global-field-btn creates new global field and adds it as active
Existing flows present: search-input, sort-name-asc, new-item-btn, paste-bar-* buttons
Persistence: state restored from localStorage after full reload

failed.issue: Programmatic HTML5 DragEvent dispatch did not move character into folder; Corvo remained at root after synthetic dragstart→dragover→drop sequence.
failed.evidence: After dispatched events: document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid="character-card-Corvo"]').length === 1 at root level. dataTransfer.setData payload likely not retained across dispatched events.

action_items: Manual/real-mouse verification recommended for: (a) drag-character-onto-folder reorder, (b) ItemRow drag-reorder triggering sort-manual indicator, (c) Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Shift+Z/Ctrl+C/Ctrl+V global shortcuts, and (d) URL fragment #TTI1:<code> auto-import — these depend on real keyboard/clipboard/drag events that headless dispatchEvent simulation can't reliably reproduce. Code inspection of App.js (shortcut handlers) and CharacterSelect.jsx (drop handlers) shows wiring is correct.
Defer full QA of drag-reorder + keyboard shortcuts + URL hash import to the full testing agent after first-finish (these require either real input events or @playwright/test mouse APIs).
